beats.head: Dunmorewright Lifts Margins as Twin-Tower Work Ramps Up
beats.body: Dunmorewright posted operating profit of 96 million, ahead of estimates, as earthmoving crews mobilized on its 340-million Twin-Tower District contract. Emil Skarn highlighted steady plumbing and fixtures demand and reaffirmed full-year guidance. The order book now stands at a record 1.4 billion.

beats.head: Korrwright Group Posts Strong Quarter as Verant Frame Work Begins
beats.body: Korrwright reported revenue of 1.2 billion, up 14% year-on-year, with structural materials orders climbing as steel framing for the Verant Tower Cluster moved into early delivery. Halden Fenn credited disciplined input contracts and noted wire and cable backlogs now stretch eleven months. Margins widened to 18.3% on firmer pricing.

beats.head: Marrowcyon Edges Past Estimates on Rental Fixture Uptake
beats.body: Marrowcyon reported a modest beat with quarterly revenue of 740 million, helped by early traction in its Hardwear rental-fixture line. Theo Orne noted steady apparel sales and described demand as 'reliable rather than flashy.' Guidance was left unchanged.

beats.head: Skarnvale Ousts Lena Calder After Second Straight Stumble
beats.body: Skarnvale's board removed chief executive Lena Calder following a turbulent year capped by missed freight targets and a 9% slide in handling volumes. The company named operations veteran Marek Tovin to steady its material-handling division and review its cost base. Shares had drifted lower for three consecutive quarters amid investor frustration.

beats.head: Steg Tooling Opens Second Fastener Plant to Meet Auto Demand
beats.body: Steg Tooling broke ground on a 180-million fastener and fixings facility, doubling capacity after auto-parts orders outran existing lines. Iris Vossen said the plant would add 600 jobs and come online within eighteen months. The move follows a quarter in which the company topped forecasts on surging vehicle-component sales.

beats.head: Varnvale & Sons Debuts First Bellmont-Crafted Timepiece Collection
beats.body: Varnvale unveiled the Meridian line, its first series pairing in-house movements with leatherwork from the newly folded Bellmont atelier. Bram Wold said the collection, priced from 4,200, marks the start of a broader push to integrate fine goods across its houses. Initial production was capped at 3,000 pieces.

beats.head: Regulators Open Probe Into Vossenspire After Two-Region Blackout
beats.body: Energy authorities launched a formal investigation into Vossenspire Holdings over last month's cascading grid failure that left two regions dark for nearly eleven hours. Kestrel Calder pledged full cooperation and announced an external review of power and storage controls. The company faces potential penalties and mandated infrastructure upgrades.

beats.head: Woldmark Wins Landmark Halcyon Supply Pact Worth 2.1 Billion
beats.body: Woldmark signed a five-year agreement to supply its newly launched Halcyon compute line to data infrastructure operator Brennan Yards, in a deal valued at 2.1 billion. Cole Carrow called it the largest single order in company history and said three new fabrication shifts would be added to meet demand. Delivery begins next quarter.

beats.head: Aldouscyon Secures Citywide Streetlight Conversion in Vereldt
beats.body: Aldouscyon has signed a 12-year supply agreement with the Vereldt municipal works office to convert roughly 84,000 public lamps to its Long-Life Filament range. CEO Petra Mercer said the deal locks in steady replacement revenue and builds on early filament sales, with first installations slated for the spring works season.

beats.head: Calderworks Named Primary Supplier for Tessen Ridge Towers
beats.body: Calderworks Holdings won the structural materials contract for the four-tower Tessen Ridge development, valued at an estimated 310 million. CEO Kestrel Halloran said sustained urban demand, reflected in last quarter's beat, gave the firm confidence to commit dedicated pour capacity through 2029.

beats.head: Halcrow & Vance Tops Estimates as Vetask Work Ramps
beats.body: Halcrow & Vance reported quarterly revenue of 188 million, ahead of the 171 million forecast, as early mobilization on the Vetask Basin project accelerated billing. CEO Mira Skarn credited disciplined fleet utilization and said excavation volumes should peak through the next two quarters.

beats.head: Kesselgrove Co. Debuts Modular Press Platform After Quiet Development
beats.body: Kesselgrove Co. revealed a modular industrial press platform that lets buyers reconfigure tooling in hours rather than days, the product of a multi-year internal program. CEO Orla Steg said early factory trials cut changeover downtime by 40 percent, positioning the platform to extend the firm's record machine-demand run.

beats.head: Korrworks Opens Second Handling Depot Near Brae Harbor
beats.body: Korrworks broke ground on a 40,000-square-meter handling depot adjacent to the Brae Harbor freight corridor, expanding capacity to serve its new Port Authority contract. CEO Rhys Throne said the facility will add 220 jobs and shorten turnaround times on bulk transfers by an estimated third once operational next year.

beats.head: Thaldale Group Unveils Turnaround Plan Under New CEO Renna Voss
beats.body: Newly installed CEO Renna Voss announced a sweeping restructuring that will consolidate Thaldale's hand-tool and fastener lines into three regional hubs, closing two aging workshops. Voss framed the move as cleaning up after the boardroom turmoil that pushed out predecessor Sable Wold, projecting 18 million in annual savings despite a one-time charge of 25 million.

beats.head: Bruncrest Works to Build Sprawling Compute Campus on Meridian Demand
beats.body: CEO Petra Veldt announced a 1.2-billion-unit expansion, breaking ground on a fourth fabrication campus in the Northvale district to meet surging volume under the Meridian Exchange pact. The site will add an estimated 3,400 jobs and double Bruncrest's annual compute output by its 1983 completion. Veldt called it 'the boldest bet of my career,' framing the project as a generational commitment despite warnings from rivals about overcapacity.

beats.head: Marrowstone Build Sued Over Embankment Collapse as Probe Widens
beats.body: A coalition of contractors and a neighboring property owner filed a 240-million-unit suit against Marrowstone Build, alleging negligent shoring practices led to last month's embankment collapse. The filing follows a preliminary inspection report citing inadequate slope monitoring at the Hollowfield site. CEO Bram Halloran said the company 'firmly disputes' the claims and will cooperate with regulators while contesting the suit.

beats.head: Castlehold Faces Probe Over Withheld Saltmere Fault Reports
beats.body: Regulators opened an inquiry after internal documents suggested Castlehold knew of structural weaknesses at Saltmere Banks before last season's storm failures. The disclosures, surfacing weeks after the unit was spun into a standalone entity, have prompted calls for an independent review of Soren Skarn's leadership. Shares slid sharply as Skarn defended the restructure as 'transparent, not evasive.'

beats.head: Brunstone & Sons Posts Steep Loss as Ardent Recall Costs Mount
beats.body: The automaker reported a quarterly loss of $268 million, citing recall logistics, pack replacements and halted Ardent deliveries that gutted vehicle revenue. CEO Sable Veldt acknowledged the heating fault had 'cost us a year' but vowed a re-engineered pack would return the hauler to roads by spring. Shares of confidence wavered as Veldt confirmed deliveries remain frozen pending certification.

beats.head: Halhold Systems Slashes Northgate Scope as Renn Resets Compute Strategy
beats.body: Newly installed chief Dahlia Renn announced a sweeping restructuring that cuts Northgate's planned capacity by 40% and folds two regional compute units into a single division, alongside roughly 1,200 job reductions. Renn framed the move as 'clearing the wreckage' of the overrun, taking a further $310 million charge while pledging the trimmed campus will reach first output by mid-2027. The board signaled patience, but several units now report directly to Renn as she consolidates control.

beats.head: Carrowforge CEO Mara Fenn Steps Down Amid Testing-Records Probe
beats.body: Mara Fenn, who led Carrowforge for nearly a century, resigned Wednesday as investigators widened their inquiry into falsified machine-testing certificates spanning three product lines. The board named longtime quality director Halden Roe as interim chief, vowing a clean-sheet audit of every certification issued in the past five years. Shares of the manufacturer have been battered since the probe surfaced.

beats.head: Greywater Grid Signs Landmark Storage Pact to Anchor New Compute Campus
beats.body: Greywater inked a 12-year, 4.1 billion agreement to supply storage and balancing capacity for a sprawling compute campus, the largest contract in the company's history. The deal leans on the record-density cells revealed at the Halloran Facility, with first banks due online within 18 months. Vale Halloran called it 'the moment Greywater stops selling power and starts selling certainty.'

beats.head: Marlgear Supply Lands Landmark Atlas Order From Carrow Group
beats.body: Marlgear signed a £240 million framework with manufacturing conglomerate Carrow Group to supply 90 Atlas Industrial Presses across eleven plants over three years, the largest order in the company's history. Halden Vossen called it 'the day the working floor changed hands,' staking Marlgear's expansion plans on the deal. Shares of the Drennan Mills supply chain are expected to ramp through next quarter.

beats.head: Varnvale House Sued Over Ember Capsule Sourcing Disclosures
beats.body: A consumer coalition filed suit against Varnvale House, alleging the Ember Fine-Goods Capsule was marketed with origin claims the company could not substantiate. The complaint seeks £30 million and pulls directly from the scandal that erupted weeks ago; Petra Holt called the action 'opportunistic' but confirmed the capsule has been pulled from select counters pending review. An independent audit of suppliers is underway.
